title: Minimal and normalized state

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Minimal and normalized state; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Learn and Reference; React 19.2; provisions applicable to Minimal and normalized state
sources.claim: State should be minimal, normalized, and free of redundant values that can be derived from props or other state during render.

canonicalExplanation.summary: State should be minimal, normalized, and free of redundant values that can be derived from props or other state during render.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A cart stores items and derives total rather than storing an independently editable total that can drift. Your goal is to refactor a component to one source of truth and explain each remaining state variable. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Props and composition; javascript events model; useState and snapshots; Props and one-way data flow. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: State should be minimal, normalized, and free of redundant values that can be derived from props or other state during render. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Caching expensive derivations differs from storing truth, nested mutation hides changes, and state shape controls update complexity. This draft therefore rejects: Every displayed value should have its own state variable.

includes: Avoid redundant, contradictory or duplicated state and derive values during render when cheap.
Refactor a component to one source of truth and explain each remaining state variable.
excludes: Caching expensive derivations differs from storing truth, nested mutation hides changes, and state shape controls update complexity.

outcomes: Refactor a component to one source of truth and explain each remaining state variable.

examples.title: Minimal and normalized state: concrete case
examples.situation: A cart stores items and derives total rather than storing an independently editable total that can drift.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A cart stores items and derives total rather than storing an independently editable total that can drift.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Store the minimal source of truth and derive dependent values to avoid inconsistent redundant state.
examples.result: The result follows because Store the minimal source of truth and derive dependent values to avoid inconsistent redundant state.
